jobs.purpose: Asynchronous offboarding fan-out. Dispatched by the PlatformUserOffboarded event listener. Revokes every active Sanctum PAT on the platform_admin guard, invalidates every JWT signed for this PlatformUser (by adding the jti to the deny-list cache), terminates every active impersonation session, and unregisters from every on-call rotation. Runs the full sequence inside 24 hours of the offboard event to satisfy the SOC2 access-removal SLA. Emits `stackra.platform_user.offboarding.access_revoked` metric with a `duration_seconds` label so a compliance dashboard can prove the SLA.
steps: Resolve the PlatformUser row (must exist, may be soft-deleted — offboarding may soft-delete before this job runs).
Iterate personal_access_tokens where tokenable_type = App\PlatformUser\Models\PlatformUser AND tokenable_id = <id>; DELETE each row (Sanctum revocation).
Query auth::JwtDenyList and INSERT jti + exp for every JWT currently outstanding for this PlatformUser (12h TTL matches the JWT ttl_seconds).

Query access::PlatformImpersonationSession WHERE impersonator_platform_user_id = <id> AND ended_at IS NULL; call access::TerminateImpersonation on each (marks ended_at + emits ImpersonationTerminated event).
If PlatformUser.on_call_rotation_key IS NOT NULL: call OnCallResolver::unregister(<key>) which posts to the external provider (PagerDuty) OR removes the row from the internal rotation table.
Emit `stackra.platform_user.offboarding.access_revoked` metric with duration_seconds label (must be < 86400 to satisfy the SLA).
Write a `platform_user.access_revoked` activity_log entry with correlation id shared with the triggering PlatformUserOffboarded event.

sla.target: revocation completed within 24 hours of PlatformUserOffboarded emission
sla.measurement: duration_seconds label on the completion metric
sla.alerting: PagerDuty page fires when a job stays in queue past 12h (half-SLA)

jobs.purpose: Hard-delete PlatformUser rows whose `deleted_at` is older than the retention hold-period (default 90 days, configurable via `platform-user.retention.hold_days`). Cascades PlatformProfile purge in the same transaction. Audit rows referencing this PlatformUser are RETAINED with an anonymised actor placeholder — audit is compliance evidence + preserves cross-tenant traceability.
steps: SELECT id FROM platform_users WHERE deleted_at IS NOT NULL AND status = 'offboarded' AND deleted_at + (config('platform-user.retention.hold_days') || ' days')::interval < now() LIMIT 100.
For each: check RevokePlatformUserAccessJob completed successfully (metric emission recorded in observability store).
If access-revocation did not complete: skip + emit `stackra.platform_user.retention.blocked` warning metric + write compliance-alert audit entry (points at the ops rotation).

Otherwise: BEGIN TRANSACTION; DELETE FROM platform_profiles WHERE id = <profile_id>; DELETE FROM platform_users WHERE id = <id>; UPDATE audits SET user_id = '[REDACTED]' + anonymise user_type where user_id = <id> (post-hard-delete audit rows keep a resolvable placeholder); COMMIT.

The Identity row is NOT touched — identity_id becomes dangling but the identity row is preserved. It may live on as a tenant User's credential OR be erased separately via identity::PurgeSoftDeletedIdentitiesJob when no downstream principals reference it.
